description_long: This is a large, multi-story apartment complex characterized by several interconnected red-brick towers arranged in a staggered formation. The central cluster of buildings is surrounded by lower, beige structures, likely serving as additional residential or communal facilities. The complex is set within a rectangular plot, bordered by paved walkways, green courtyards, and a main road. Numerous windows are evenly distributed across the facades, and the buildings feature flat roofs, some of which may be accessible. The overall layout suggests a planned residential development, possibly with shared outdoor spaces and amenities. The architectural style is utilitarian, with a focus on function and density, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century urban housing.
description: A large, red-brick apartment complex with multiple interconnected towers and surrounding beige buildings, set within a landscaped urban block. The structure features flat roofs, numerous windows, and communal outdoor areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance area, central towers, and landscaped courtyards.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the red-brick towers and the arrangement of beige auxiliary buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades of the towers and the adjacent road.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the full width of the complex and the distribution of the surrounding structures.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with even spacing and alignment across all facades, contributing to a sense of symmetry and order.
